DetectionRule.Name: Large File Transfer to External Network
DetectionRule.Description: Detects large file transfers from corporate endpoints to external networks, indicative of data exfiltration attempts. Analyzes network traffic patterns, file sizes, and destination IPs to identify suspicious outbound data flows.
DetectionRule.MITREMapping: T1041 - Exfiltration Over C2 Channel, T1039 - Data from Network Shared Drive
DetectionRule.ThreatActors: APT29, APT41, Lazarus, FIN7, Ransomware Groups

RemediationSteps.Step: IMMEDIATELY: Isolate affected device from network
Block external IP addresses at firewall
Identify data that was potentially exfiltrated
Preserve network logs for forensic analysis
Reset credentials for compromised account
Implement DLP policies to prevent future exfiltration

FalsePositiveReduction.Condition: Exclude known backup and sync services
Time-based: Allow larger transfers during business hours
Whitelist approved external services by IP

DetectionRule.Name: Cloud Storage Mass Upload Detection
DetectionRule.Description: Detects mass upload of files to cloud storage services (OneDrive, Google Drive, Dropbox, Box, etc.) that may indicate data exfiltration. Analyzes file operations and sync client behavior to identify unusual upload patterns.
DetectionRule.MITREMapping: T1567.002 - Exfiltration to Cloud Storage: Cloud Storage, T1567.001 - Exfiltration to Cloud Storage: OneDrive
DetectionRule.ThreatActors: APT29, APT41, FIN7, Ransomware Groups, Insider Threat

RemediationSteps.Step: Revoke cloud storage access tokens immediately
Identify which files were uploaded to cloud storage
Download and preserve uploaded files as evidence
Remove files from cloud storage and disable affected accounts
Review cloud storage access logs for additional exfiltration
Implement Cloud Access Security Broker (CASB) controls

FalsePositiveReduction.Condition: Exclude authorized backup and migration tools
Exclude known business processes requiring cloud uploads
Time-based: Allow larger uploads during maintenance windows
User-based: Exclude data migration specialists during projects

DetectionRule.Name: DNS Tunneling Data Exfiltration
DetectionRule.Description: Detects DNS tunneling techniques used to exfiltrate data by encoding information in DNS queries. Monitors for unusual DNS query patterns, high volumes of DNS requests, and suspicious subdomain structures.
DetectionRule.MITREMapping: T1048.003 - Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol: DNS, T1071.004 - Application Layer Protocol: DNS

RemediationSteps.Step: Block suspicious DNS domains at DNS resolver level
Isolate affected device immediately
Analyze DNS query logs to determine data being exfiltrated
Check for malware that implements DNS tunneling
Implement DNS query length and frequency restrictions
Deploy DNS security monitoring and filtering (e.g., Cisco Umbrella)

FalsePositiveReduction.Condition: Exclude internal DNS servers and resolvers
Exclude legitimate applications with long DNS queries (CDNs)

DetectionRule.Name: Archive Creation Before Exfiltration
DetectionRule.Description: Detects creation of archive files (ZIP, RAR, 7Z) followed by rapid external transfer, which is a common precursor to data exfiltration. Identifies compression of multiple files before transmission outside the network.
DetectionRule.MITREMapping: T1560.001 - Archive Collected Data: Archive via Utility, T1041 - Exfiltration Over C2 Channel

RemediationSteps.Step: Check archive contents for sensitive data
Identify destination of exfiltrated data
Block external IP addresses and domains
Review access controls for data that was archived
Implement DLP policies to detect archive creation
Monitor for additional exfiltration attempts

FalsePositiveReduction.Condition: Exclude authorized backup and archiving processes
Exclude file compression for email attachments
Time-based: Focus on unusual patterns outside business hours
Size-based: Require minimum archive size threshold
